hypothesis: For prompts with fixed layer counts, replacing CHROMA’s discrete material–thickness pair tokenization with a factorized decoder that predicts a discrete material and a continuous thickness per layer and is trained end-to-end with a differentiable TMM-derived sequence-level CIE76 loss will reduce the mean held-out ∆E by at least 30% compared to the current token-level cross-entropy baseline at equal encoder and decoder sizes.
